title: ericameria cooperi
intro: Ericameria cooperi, also known as Coyote Brush or Bigelow's Goldenrod, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its small yellow flowers and strong, pungent scent. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Ericameria cooperi include reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and providing antioxidant support, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health concerns. Ericameria cooperi can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
